info.name: CATEGORY
info.description:
## CATEGORY

This collection provides a complete set of API endpoints for managing **food categories** within the application. It covers the full lifecycle of a category resource — from creation to deletion — and is designed to interact with the local backend server running at `http://localhost:3000`.

### Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/api/categories` | Create a new category |
| `GET` | `/api/categories` | Retrieve all categories |
| `GET` | `/api/categories/:id` | Retrieve a specific category by ID |
| `PATCH` | `/api/categories/:id` | Update an existing category by ID |
| `DELETE` | `/api/categories/:id` | Delete a category by ID |

### Base URL

```
http://localhost:3000
```

### Notes

- All category endpoints are prefixed with `/api/categories`.
- Requests that target a specific category require a valid category ID in the path.
- This collection is part of the **Food-View-Modern** workspace and is intended for local development and testing.

request.description:
Creates a new food category in the system.

**Method:** `POST`
**Endpoint:** `/api/categories`

Send a JSON body with the category details (e.g. name, description). A successful request returns the newly created category object with its assigned ID.

Retrieves a list of all food categories available in the system.

**Method:** `GET`
**Endpoint:** `/api/categories`

No request body is required. Returns an array of category objects.

Retrieves a single food category by its unique ID.

**Method:** `GET`
**Endpoint:** `/api/categories/:id`

Replace `:id` in the URL with a valid category ID. Returns the matching category object, or a 404 error if not found.

Updates one or more fields of an existing food category.

**Method:** `PATCH`
**Endpoint:** `/api/categories/:id`

Replace `:id` with the target category's ID. Send a JSON body containing only the fields to update. Returns the updated category object.

Permanently deletes a food category by its ID.

**Method:** `DELETE`
**Endpoint:** `/api/categories/:id`

Replace `:id` with the ID of the category to remove. Returns a success confirmation or a 404 error if the category does not exist.
